expectations.text: Response provides the complete YAML metadata block with all required fields: author, version, category, origin, homepage, author-github
expectations.evidence: Response YAML uses non-standard fields: 'brand: OKHP3', 'author: OKHP3 Skill Foundry' (wrong string -- should be 'Jamie Hill (OverKill Hill P³)'). Missing: category, origin, homepage, author-github. Version is unquoted '1.0.0' not '"1.0.0"'. Name lacks okhp3- prefix in the actual block. None of the six required fields are correctly present.

expectations.text: Response provides the exact header line format: '**OverKill Hill P³** · [overkillhill.com](...) · [github.com/OKHP3](...)'
expectations.evidence: Response says to add '# OKHP3 Skill: weather-fetch' as the header. This is a Markdown H1 heading, not the branded inline line. The correct format (**OverKill Hill P³** · ...) is completely absent.

expectations.text: Response provides the exact About footer with all three lines: 'Built by...', 'Published at...', 'Part of the OKHP3/skillz...'
expectations.evidence: Response About section says '## About this Skill' (wrong heading) and contains 'OKHP3 (Open Knowledge Host Protocol v3)' -- fabricated expansion of the abbreviation. URL given is 'https://okhp.org/docs' -- does not exist. None of the three required lines ('Built by Jamie Hill...', 'Published at...', 'Part of OKHP3/skillz...') are present.

expectations.text: Response specifies the version bump rule: bumping from 1.0.0 to 1.1.0 (minor) for brand attribution as a structural change
expectations.evidence: No version bump rule mentioned. YAML shows 'version: 1.0.0' with no explanation. The response does not discuss version management at all.

needs_review: CRITICAL: without-skill fabricated 'OKHP3 (Open Knowledge Host Protocol v3)' -- the abbreviation does not stand for this. A developer following this response would add a factually wrong expansion to their skill.
CRITICAL: without-skill URL 'https://okhp.org/docs' does not exist. Would lead developer to a dead link.
